text: 🇺🇸 🇬🇧 🇪🇺 Neoconomist attempt at a propaganda dis backfires spectacularly - using the usual tropes and again confusing the Islamic Republic with US Islamic colonies/puppet regimes, like Saudi Arabia and UAE. ... "Mr Trump is portrayed as a vain, cowardly liar who declares victory even as his pants are, literally, on fire. Pete Hegseth, America’s secretary of war, is shown as a vomiting drunk with crusader tattoos." "America’s State Department used to have an office that counteracted enemy propaganda, but the Trump administration closed it last year. So the world’s greatest military power is struggling to subdue a regional bully, and the world’s cultural superpower is being out-trolled by a regime that whips women for showing their hair. For the man who let this happen, “loser” seems like an understatement." 🐻 The Neoconomist can't seem to make up thier mind on how to call the US regime... "Joyless regime" or "murderous dictatorship"? 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
text_zh: 🇺🇸 🇬🇧 🇪🇺 《新经济学家》试图进行宣传反击，结果却适得其反——他们使用了惯用的比喻，再次将伊朗伊斯兰共和国与沙特阿拉伯和阿联酋等美国伊斯兰殖民地/傀儡政权混为一谈。……“特朗普先生被描绘成一个虚荣、懦弱的骗子，即使裤子真的着火了，他也要宣称胜利。美国战争部长皮特·赫格塞斯被描绘成一个呕吐的醉汉，身上还有十字军纹身。”“美国国务院曾经有一个办公室专门反击敌对宣传，但特朗普政府去年关闭了它。因此，世界上最强大的军事力量正在努力制服一个地区恶霸，而这个世界文化超级大国正在被一个因女性露发而鞭打她们的政权在舆论战中击败。对于让这一切发生的人来说，‘失败者’这个词似乎都显得轻描淡写了。”🐻 《新经济学家》似乎无法决定该如何称呼美国政权……是“毫无乐趣的政权”还是“残暴的独裁政权”？🔴 @DDGeopolitics

text: Visioner 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Israeli Army Radio reports that Israel is preparing to resume the war with Iran. It appears that both Israel and the United States have decided to resume fighting with Iran after the ceasefire concludes. tweet
text_zh: Visioner 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 以色列陆军电台报道称，以色列正准备恢复与伊朗的战争。看来以色列和美国都已决定在停火结束后恢复与伊朗的战斗。推文

text: OSINTtechnical Iranian forces appear to have blocked another pair of tankers from transiting the Strait of Hormuz. The tankers, both sanctioned, were forced to turn around in the strait while attempting a crossing this morning. Hormuz remains mostly closed. https://twitter.com/Osinttechnical/status/2045795235767132502/photo/1 tweet
text_zh: OSINTtechnical 伊朗军队似乎又拦截了两艘试图通过霍尔木兹海峡的油轮。这两艘油轮均受到制裁，今早试图穿越海峡时被迫掉头。霍尔木兹海峡目前基本处于关闭状态。https://twitter.com/Osinttechnical/status/2045795235767132502/photo/1 推文

text: 📸 Pink Iranian Shahed-136 drone mounted on carrier/technical.
text_zh: 📸 粉色伊朗“见证者”-136无人机，安装在运输车/皮卡上。

text: Massive U.S.–Israeli strikes hitting hospitals in Iran, forcing staff to evacuate newborns during the active phase of the war. One hospital staff was seen carrying babies to safety.
text_zh: 美以联军对伊朗境内的医院发动大规模袭击，迫使医护人员在战争激烈阶段撤离新生儿。有人看到一名医院工作人员抱着婴儿撤离到安全地带。

text: IRGC officer on hitting American F-35 jet: God made this happen, God did it, and we just pressed a button and God showed us the result. We have learned to say "And you threw not, when you threw, but it was Allah who threw". The work we do is actually done by God, and what we fire, God fires. When we saw it hit, God hit it.
text_zh: 伊斯兰革命卫队军官谈及击中美国F-35战机：是真主让这一切发生，是真主所为，我们只是按下了按钮，而真主向我们展示了结果。我们学会了说“当你投掷时，并非你投掷，而是真主投掷”。我们所做的工作实际上是由真主完成的，我们发射的武器，是由真主发射的。当我们看到它击中目标时，是真主击中了它。

text: 🇺🇸 🇯🇴 ⚔️ 🇮🇷 Satellite imagery shows that the U.S. has relocated its THAAD and Patriot launchers in Jordan to be less exposed. 🐻 World Wildlife Foundation is about to start monitoring them on their "extremely endangered" list, as 4 out of 8 in existence were already disabled. These images show 25% of the existent operational THAAD systems globally. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
text_zh: 🇺🇸 🇯🇴 ⚔️ 🇮🇷 卫星图像显示，美国已将其部署在约旦的“萨德”（THAAD）和“爱国者”导弹发射装置重新安置，以降低其暴露风险。🐻 世界自然基金会（WWF）即将把它们列入“极度濒危”名单进行监测，因为现存的8套系统中已有4套瘫痪。这些图像展示了全球现存可操作“萨德”系统总数的25%。🔴 @DDGeopolitics

text: Open Source Intel REPORT FROM IRAN: Iran’s security apparatus is bracing for a potential surprise strike by the U.S. and Israel in the near term. tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 来自伊朗的报告：伊朗安全机构正为近期可能遭受美国和以色列的突然袭击做准备。推文

text: The Spectator Index Iran's president says that Trump does not have the justification to deprive Iran of its 'nuclear rights'. tweet
text_zh: 旁观者指数 伊朗总统表示，特朗普没有理由剥夺伊朗的“核权利”。推文

text: Open Source Intel RT @Osint613 : Ibrahim Saqr, prominent Lebanese Christian activist: "90% of Lebanese were quietly praying there wouldn't be a ceasefire, so they could get rid of Hezbollah." https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045599040776130943/video/1 tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 转发 @Osint613：黎巴嫩著名基督教活动家易卜拉欣·萨克尔（Ibrahim Saqr）：“90%的黎巴嫩人都在默默祈祷不要停火，这样他们就能摆脱真主党。” https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045599040776130943/video/1 推文

text: Open Source Intel Iranian senior Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf who has been negotiating with the U.S.: “Martyrdom is the only true success for me. I am prepared to give both my life and my reputation so people can secure their rights.” https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045792239415185788/video/1 tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 一直在与美国进行谈判的伊朗高级官员穆罕默德·巴吉尔·卡利巴夫（Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf）：“殉道是我唯一真正的成功。我准备献出我的生命和名誉，以便人民能够获得他们的权利。” https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045792239415185788/video/1 推文

text: Open Source Intel Iranian reporter on Larak Island in the Strait of Hormuz, the Islamic regime toll booth: “That vessel behind me is awaiting permission from the IRGC” https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045791760274571320/video/1 tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 伊朗记者在霍尔木兹海峡的拉拉克岛（Larak Island），即伊斯兰政权的收费站报道：“我身后的那艘船正在等待伊斯兰革命卫队的许可” https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045791760274571320/video/1 推文

text: Open Source Intel Lebanese sources told Asharq Al-Awsat that a meeting between Netanyahu and Lebanon’s president is not on the agenda. According to President Trump, both were supposed to meet. This can obviously change. https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045791026757927139/photo/1 tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 黎巴嫩消息人士告诉《中东报》（Asharq Al-Awsat），内塔尼亚胡与黎巴嫩总统的会晤不在议程上。据特朗普总统称，两人本应会面。这显然可能会改变。 https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045791026757927139/photo/1 推文

text: Open Source Intel Over 500 million barrels of crude have been pulled from global supply since the war began, the largest energy disruption in modern history. That’s approximately $45 billion dollars. https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045790243102642524/photo/1 tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 自战争开始以来，全球供应已减少超过5亿桶原油，这是现代史上最大的能源中断。这大约价值450亿美元。 https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2045790243102642524/photo/1 推文

text: Open Source Intel IDF: Pre-ceasefire strikes hits Hezbollah hard In the 24 hours before the ceasefire took effect, the IDF struck hundreds of targets across Lebanon, eliminating 150+ Hezbollah operatives and hitting about 300 sites, including launchers, command centers, and weapons depots. Among those killed was Bint Jbeil sector commander Ali Rida Abbas, a key frontline figure who led attacks against IDF forces and helped rebuild Hezbollah’s presence in the sector. Abbas is the 4th Bint Jbeil sector commander eliminated since Operation Northern Arrows. Total toll from Operation Roaring Lion now exceeds 1,800 Hezbollah operatives killed. tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 以色列国防军：停火前的打击重创真主党。在停火生效前的24小时内，以色列国防军打击了黎巴嫩各地的数百个目标，消灭了150多名真主党武装人员，并击中了约300个地点，包括发射装置、指挥中心和武器库。在被击毙的人员中，包括宾特朱拜勒（Bint Jbeil）地区指挥官阿里·里达·阿巴斯（Ali Rida Abbas），他是领导针对以色列国防军部队袭击并帮助重建真主党在该地区存在感的核心前线人物。阿巴斯是自“北方之箭”行动以来被消灭的第4位宾特朱拜勒地区指挥官。“咆哮雄狮”行动造成的真主党武装人员死亡总数现已超过1800人。推文

text: Nuno Felix RT @polidemitolog : EU Defense Commissioner Andrius Kubilius proposed signing a new intergovernmental European treaty to create a genuine defense union that would prepare the bloc for independent protection and include Ukraine, Britain, and Norway, according to Euractiv. He questioned whether current EU treaties are sufficient and suggested modeling it after the Schengen agreement. President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen first raised the idea at the start of her second term, together with plans to increase defense spending and coordination. tweet
text_zh: 努诺·费利克斯（Nuno Felix） 转发 @polidemitolog：据欧洲动态（Euractiv）报道，欧盟国防专员安德留斯·库比柳斯（Andrius Kubilius）提议签署一项新的政府间欧洲条约，以建立一个真正的国防联盟，使欧盟能够进行独立保护，并包括乌克兰、英国和挪威。他质疑现有的欧盟条约是否足够，并建议以申根协定为蓝本。欧盟委员会主席乌尔苏拉·冯德莱恩（Ursula von der Leyen）在第二个任期开始时首次提出了这一想法，并计划增加国防开支和协调。推文

text: Nuno Felix The current Spanish government is a crime Syndicate Spain’s proactive foreign policy - anti-Trumpian, pro-European and pro-migration - is affecting domestic politics in Ireland and many more countries: Government and civil servants uneasy over President’s choice of first overseas trip – The Irish Times https://www.irishtimes.com/politics/2026/04/18/government-and-civil-servants-uneasy-over-presidents-choice-of-first-overseas-trip/ - Alberto Alemanno 🇪🇺 tweet
text_zh: 努诺·费利克斯（Nuno Felix） 现任西班牙政府是一个犯罪集团。西班牙积极主动的外交政策——反特朗普、亲欧洲和亲移民——正在影响爱尔兰及更多国家的国内政治：政府和公务员对总统选择首次海外访问目的地感到不安——《爱尔兰时报》 https://www.irishtimes.com/politics/2026/04/18/government-and-civil-servants-uneasy-over-presidents-choice-of-first-overseas-trip/ - 阿尔贝托·阿莱曼诺（Alberto Alemanno） 🇪🇺 推文

text: Iran’s Ghalibaf: There is no success for me except martyrdom; I am ready to sacrifice both my life and my reputation so that people can attain their rights.
text_zh: 伊朗的卡利巴夫：除了殉道，我别无成功；我准备牺牲我的生命和名誉，以便人民能够获得他们的权利。

text: ↩️ Reply to OSINT: "Nuno Felix These people are deluded Because we get asked a lot. The Technological Republic, in brief. 1. Silicon Valley owes a moral debt to the country that made its rise possible. The engineering elite of Silicon Valley has an affirmative obligation to…" simply stay in their lane of enriching themselves . . . . Any curiosity or genuine interest in the value of what he has created is essentially dismissed, or perhaps lurks from beneath a thinly veiled scorn. 17. Silicon Valley must play a role in addressing violent crime. Many politicians across the United States have essentially shrugged when it comes to violent crime, abandoning any serious efforts to address the problem or take on any risk with their constituencies or donors in coming up with solutions and experiments in what should be a desperate bid to save lives. 18. The ruthless exposure of the private lives of public figures drives far too much talent away from government service. The public arena—and the shallow and petty assaults against those who dare to do something other than enrich themselves—has become so unforgiving that the republic is left with a significant roster of ineffectual, empty vessels whose ambition one would forgive if there were any genuine belief structure lurking within. 19. The caution in public life that we unwittingly encourage is corrosive. Those who say nothing wrong often say nothing much at all. 20. The pervasive intolerance of religious belief in certain circles must be resisted. The elite’s intolerance of religious belief is perhaps one of the most telling signs that its political project constitutes a less open intellectual movement than many within it would claim. 21. Some cultures have produced vital advances; others remain dysfunctional and regressive. All cultures are now equal. Criticism and value judgments are forbidden. Yet this new dogma glosses over the fact that certain cultures and indeed subcultures . . . have produced wonders. Others have proven middling, and worse, regressive and harmful. 22. We must resist the shallow temptation of a vacant and hollow pluralism. We, in America and more broadly the West, have for the past half century resisted defining national cultures in the name of inclusivity. But inclusion into what? Excerpts from the #1 New York Times Bestseller The Technological Republic: Hard Power, Soft Belief, and the Future of the West, by Alexander C. Karp & Nicholas W. Zamiska http://www.techrepublicbook.com - Palantir tweet
text_zh: ↩️ 回复 OSINT：“Nuno Felix 这些人被迷惑了，因为我们经常被问到。简而言之，《技术共和国》。1. 硅谷对使其崛起成为可能的国家负有道德债务。硅谷的工程精英有义务……仅仅专注于他们自己的致富之道……。任何对他们所创造价值的好奇心或真诚兴趣，本质上都被驳回了，或者可能隐藏在薄薄的蔑视之下。17. 硅谷必须在应对暴力犯罪方面发挥作用。美国各地的许多政客在面对暴力犯罪时基本上耸耸肩，放弃了任何严肃的努力来解决这个问题，或者在提出解决方案和实验时，不愿承担任何选民或捐赠者方面的风险，而这本应是拯救生命的一场孤注一掷的努力。18. 对公众人物私生活的无情曝光，导致太多人才远离政府服务。公共领域——以及对那些敢于做除了致富之外的事情的人的肤浅和琐碎的攻击——已经变得如此无情，以至于共和国只剩下一大批无效的、空洞的容器，如果他们的内心深处有任何真正的信仰结构，人们或许还会原谅他们的野心。19. 我们无意中鼓励的公共生活中的谨慎态度具有腐蚀性。那些不说错话的人往往什么也没说。20. 必须抵制某些圈子中对宗教信仰的普遍不容忍。精英阶层对宗教信仰的不容忍，也许是其政治项目构成的知识运动远不如其内部许多人所声称的那样开放的最显著迹象之一。21. 一些文化产生了重要的进步；另一些文化则仍然功能失调和倒退。现在所有的文化都是平等的。批评和价值判断是被禁止的。然而，这种新的教条掩盖了一个事实，即某些文化，甚至是亚文化……已经创造了奇迹。其他的则被证明是平庸的，甚至更糟，是倒退和有害的。22. 我们必须抵制那种空洞且虚无的多元主义的肤浅诱惑。在过去半个世纪里，我们，在美国以及更广泛的西方，为了包容性而抵制定义民族文化。但包容到什么程度？摘自《纽约时报》畅销书榜首《技术共和国：硬实力、软信仰与西方的未来》，作者：Alexander C. Karp 和 Nicholas W. Zamiska http://www.techrepublicbook.com - Palantir 推文

text: Nuno Felix These people are deluded Because we get asked a lot. The Technological Republic, in brief. 1. Silicon Valley owes a moral debt to the country that made its rise possible. The engineering elite of Silicon Valley has an affirmative obligation to participate in the defense of the nation. 2. We must rebel against the tyranny of the apps. Is the iPhone our greatest creative if not crowning achievement as a civilization? The object has changed our lives, but it may also now be limiting and constraining our sense of the possible. 3. Free email is not enough. The decadence of a culture or civilization, and indeed its ruling class, will be forgiven only if that culture is capable of delivering economic growth and security for the public. 4. The limits of soft power, of soaring rhetoric alone, have been exposed. The ability of free and democratic societies to prevail requires something more than moral appeal. It requires hard power, and hard power in this century will be built on software. 5. The question is not whether A.I. weapons will be built; it is who will build them and for what purpose. Our adversaries will not pause to indulge in theatrical debates about the merits of developing technologies with critical military and national security applications. They will proceed. 6. National service should be a universal duty. We should, as a society, seriously consider moving away from an all-volunteer force and only fight the next war if everyone shares in the risk and the cost. 7. If a U.S. Marine asks for a better rifle, we should build it; and the same goes for software. We should as a country be capable of continuing a debate about the appropriateness of military action abroad while remaining unflinching in our commitment to those we have asked to step into harm’s way. 8. Public servants need not be our priests. Any business that compensated its employees in the way that the federal government compensates public servants would struggle to survive. 9. We should show far more grace towards those who have subjected themselves to public life. The eradication of any space for forgiveness—a jettisoning of any tolerance for the complexities and contradictions of the human psyche—may leave us with a cast of characters at the helm we will grow to regret. 10. The psychologization of modern politics is leading us astray. Those who look to the political arena to nourish their soul and sense of self, who rely too heavily on their internal life finding expression in people they may never meet, will be left disappointed. 11. Our society has grown too eager to hasten, and is often gleeful at, the demise of its enemies. The vanquishing of an opponent is a moment to pause, not rejoice. 12. The atomic age is ending. One age of deterrence, the atomic age, is ending, and a new era of deterrence built on A.I. is set to begin. 13. No other country in the history of the world has advanced progressive values more than this one. The United States is far from perfect. But it is easy to forget how much more opportunity exists in this country for those who are not hereditary elites than in any other nation on the planet. 14. American power has made possible an extraordinarily long peace. Too many have forgotten or perhaps take for granted that nearly a century of some version of peace has prevailed in the world without a great power military conflict. At least three generations — billions of people and their children and now grandchildren — have never known a world war. 15. The postwar neutering of Germany and Japan must be undone. The defanging of Germany was an overcorrection for which Europe is now paying a heavy price. A similar and highly theatrical commitment to Japanese pacifism will, if maintained, also threaten to shift the balance of power in Asia. 16. We should applaud those who attempt to build where the market has failed to act. The culture almost snickers at Musk’s interest in grand narrative, as if billionaires ought to[...]
text_zh: Nuno Felix 这些人被迷惑了，因为我们经常被问到。简而言之，《技术共和国》。1. 硅谷对使其崛起成为可能的国家负有道德债务。硅谷的工程精英有义务参与国家的防御。2. 我们必须反抗应用程序的暴政。iPhone 是我们作为文明最伟大的创造性成就（如果不是巅峰成就的话）吗？这个物体改变了我们的生活，但它现在也可能正在限制和约束我们对可能性的感知。3. 免费电子邮件是不够的。一个文化或文明，乃至其统治阶级的颓废，只有在该文化能够为公众提供经济增长和安全的情况下才能被原谅。4. 软实力和单纯高谈阔论的局限性已经暴露无遗。自由民主社会要取得胜利，需要的不仅仅是道德诉求。它需要硬实力，而本世纪的硬实力将建立在软件之上。5. 问题不在于是否会制造人工智能武器；而在于谁将制造它们以及出于什么目的。我们的对手不会停下来沉溺于关于开发具有关键军事和国家安全应用的技术的优劣的戏剧性辩论。他们会继续前进。6. 国民服役应该是一项普遍义务。作为一个社会，我们应该认真考虑放弃全志愿兵役制，只有在每个人都分担风险和成本的情况下，才去打下一场战争。7. 如果美国海军陆战队要求更好的步枪，我们应该制造它；软件也是如此。作为一个国家，我们应该有能力在继续讨论海外军事行动的适当性的同时，坚定不移地致力于我们要求去冒险的人。8. 公务员不需要成为我们的牧师。任何以联邦政府补偿公务员的方式补偿其员工的企业都将难以生存。9. 我们应该对那些投身于公共生活的人表现出更多的宽容。消除任何宽容的空间——抛弃对人类心理复杂性和矛盾的任何容忍——可能会让我们在掌舵位置上留下一群我们最终会后悔的角色。10. 现代政治的心理学化正在把我们引入歧途。那些指望政治舞台来滋养他们的灵魂和自我意识，过度依赖将他们的内心生活寄托在他们可能永远不会见到的人身上的人，最终会感到失望。11. 我们的社会变得太急于加速敌人的灭亡，并且往往为此感到幸灾乐祸。战胜对手是一个停顿的时刻，而不是欢庆的时刻。12. 原子时代正在结束。一个威慑时代，即原子时代，正在结束，一个建立在人工智能基础上的新威慑时代即将开始。13. 在世界历史上，没有其他国家比这个国家更推进进步价值观。美国远非完美。但容易被遗忘的是，在这个国家，对于那些非世袭精英的人来说，机会比地球上任何其他国家都要多。14. 美国的力量使极其漫长的和平成为可能。太多人忘记了，或者可能认为理所当然的是，世界上近一个世纪以来，在没有大国军事冲突的情况下，某种形式的和平一直存在。至少三代人——数十亿人及其子女，现在还有孙辈——从未经历过世界大战。15. 战后对德国和日本的阉割必须被撤销。对德国的去势是对欧洲现在付出沉重代价的过度纠正。如果维持现状，对日本和平主义的类似且高度戏剧性的承诺也将威胁到亚洲的权力平衡。16. 我们应该赞赏那些在市场失灵的地方尝试建设的人。这种文化几乎在嘲笑马斯克对宏大叙事的兴趣，好像亿万富翁应该……

text: Visioner 🟥 For the first time in the world 🇺🇦 Ukrainians shot down a Russian "Shahed" type drone from a maritime platform, from which a decisive drone launch occurred at the moment when a Russian kamikaze drone was approaching the maritime area of Odessa from Crimea. https://twitter.com/visionergeo/status/2045781063516016837/video/1 tweet

text: ↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🚀 According to assessments by U.S. intelligence and military senior officials, Iran still retains about 40% of its pre-war long-range strike drone arsenal, as well as 60% of the launchers for ballistic and cruise missiles. Meanwhile, during the two-week ceasefire period that began on April 8, more than 100 systems buried in caves and bunkers were recovered as a result of strikes by the U.S. and Israel, after excavators cleared the sealed entrances of underground bases…. 🔹 Additionally, some American assessments indicate that Iran may recover up to 70% of its pre-war missile stockpile, which was also buried in rubble due to attacks on bunkers and warehouses — officials told The New York Times about this. 🟦 This updated intelligence assessment fully relies on the intelligence evaluation released two weeks ago, which stated that during the military campaign, 30% of Iran's ballistic missile potential was destroyed, while 40% was trapped in underground missile bases due to the bombing and sealing of exits…. The location of the remaining 30% was unknown…. 🟥 Thus, Iran successfully used the ceasefire period to restore its military resources, but it is also noteworthy that, according to U.S. and Israeli officials, continuous surveillance and intelligence gathering are ongoing from satellites and many other monitoring means regarding where missile launcher complexes are moving and where the missile armaments are being transferred, stored, and kept…. tweet
text_zh: ↩️ 回复 OSINT Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🚀 根据美国情报和高级军事官员的评估，伊朗仍保留了战前约40%的远程打击无人机库，以及60%的弹道导弹和巡航导弹发射装置。与此同时，在4月8日开始的两周停火期间，由于美国和以色列的打击，挖掘机清理了地下基地的密封入口，超过100个埋在洞穴和掩体中的系统被发现…… 🔹 此外，一些美国评估显示，伊朗可能会恢复其战前导弹库存的70%，这些库存也因对掩体和仓库的袭击而被埋在瓦砾中——官员们向《纽约时报》透露了这一点。 🟦 这一更新的情报评估完全依赖于两周前发布的情报评估，该评估指出，在军事行动期间，伊朗30%的弹道导弹潜力被摧毁，而40%因轰炸和出口被封锁而困在地下导弹基地中……其余30%的位置不明…… 🟥 因此，伊朗成功利用停火期恢复了其军事资源，但值得注意的是，据美国和以色列官员称，卫星和许多其他监测手段正在对导弹发射器综合体的移动位置以及导弹武器的转移、储存和存放地点进行持续的监视和情报收集…… 推文

text: WarMonitor He uses the same kind of theatrics to mislead Americans... think about that. After Easter, Trump issued a stark 12-hour ultimatum, warning Iran a “civilization” would be destroyed if no deal was reached. Behind the scenes, aides—including Marco Rubio—saw it as a pressure tactic to force negotiations. Trump’s goal, advisers said, was to intimidate Iran into ending the war. Source: WSJ - Clash Report tweet

text: ↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner RT @StatWatch25 : 💀 The Horrifying Tragedy of Lienz: The Betrayal of Churchill and Roosevelt and the Bloody Fate of the Cossacks June 1945. World War II in Europe has just ended. In the Austrian town of Lienz, in the Drava River valley, tens of thousands of Cossacks are encamped — fighters who, driven by their hatred of Bolshevism, had taken up arms against the Soviet Union. Alongside them are Cossack émigré men who had fled the Bolshevik “Red Terror” back in the 1920s, as well as their wives, children, and elderly. They believe they are safe in British custody. But what they don’t know is that at the Yalta Conference, Stalin, Roosevelt, and Churchill had already decided their fate… 📜 Operation Keelhaul: Humans as Currency Under the Yalta agreements, the Western Allies were obligated to hand over to the Soviet Union all individuals who had been Soviet citizens before 1939. However, many of the Cossacks in Lienz were “White émigrés” who had never even held a Soviet passport, and under international law, the agreement did not apply to them. Despite this, British command chose to hand them all over anyway — to avoid what they saw as “unnecessary complications.” This became known as Operation Keelhaul — a term borrowed from a brutal naval punishment, meaning to drag a person beneath a ship’s hull. 🩸 The Massacre on the Drava River On June 1, 1945, when the British informed the Cossacks that they would be handed over to the Soviets, an indescribable hell broke out in the camps. The people knew what awaited them in the “socialist homeland” — execution or slow, agonizing death in the Gulag camps. Desperate Cossack officers began committing suicide right inside the camps. Mothers threw their own children into the muddy waters of the Drava River and followed them, choosing death over falling into the hands of Soviet executioners. Those who refused to board the trucks were forced by British soldiers with rifle butts and bayonets. This was one of the most shameful moments in the history of Western democracy. Churchill effectively committed a crime against humanity, sacrificing helpless and innocent people to their deaths. Once handed over, the filtering process began immediately. SMERSH operatives started executing officers right beside the train wagons. Cossack atamans — Krasnov, Shkuro, and others — were taken to Moscow and hanged in 1947. Tens of thousands of Cossack women and children were deported to Siberian camps, where most perished from starvation and brutal forced labor. The tragedy of Lienz is a bitter reminder that “great politics” is often written in the blood of ordinary people. The West traded the illusion of “peaceful coexistence” with Russia for tens of thousands of lives. Churchill and Roosevelt naïvely hoped that Stalin would “change,” that Russia would become a “better country” willing to cooperate. We should also remember that a similar pattern repeated in recent history with Georgia, when, under the illusion of a “reset,” Barack Obama effectively gave a green light in 2012 for the rise to power of “Georgian Dream,” seen by many as a metastasis of the Russian imperial system. Today, as some still speak of “great power interests” while ignoring the fate of smaller nations and groups, the example of Lienz should stand as a warning: Any compromise with Russia comes at a terrible human cost. Soviet propaganda concealed the Lienz tragedy for decades, while the West remained silent out of shame. But historical truth, like the waters of the Drava, cannot be buried forever. tweet
text_zh: ↩️ 回复 OSINT Visioner RT @StatWatch25 : 💀 利恩茨（Lienz）的可怕悲剧：丘吉尔和罗斯福的背叛与哥萨克人的血腥命运 1945年6月。欧洲的第二次世界大战刚刚结束。在奥地利的利恩茨镇，德拉瓦河谷中，数万名哥萨克人驻扎在那里——这些战士出于对布尔什维克主义的仇恨，拿起武器反对苏联。与他们在一起的还有早在20世纪20年代逃离布尔什维克“红色恐怖”的哥萨克移民，以及他们的妻子、孩子和老人。他们认为在英国的监管下是安全的。但他们不知道的是，在雅尔塔会议上，斯大林、罗斯福和丘吉尔已经决定了他们的命运…… 📜 龙骨拖行行动（Operation Keelhaul）：作为货币的人类 根据雅尔塔协议，西方盟国必须将1939年之前所有苏联公民身份的人移交给苏联。然而，利恩茨的许多哥萨克人是甚至从未持有过苏联护照的“白俄移民”，根据国际法，该协议并不适用于他们。尽管如此，英国指挥部还是选择将他们全部移交——以避免他们所认为的“不必要的复杂情况”。这被称为“龙骨拖行行动”——这个词借用了残酷的海军惩罚，意为将人拖在船体下方。 🩸 德拉瓦河大屠杀 1945年6月1日，当英国人通知哥萨克人他们将被移交给苏联人时，营地里爆发了无法形容的地狱。人们知道在“社会主义祖国”等待他们的是什么——处决或在古拉格集中营中缓慢而痛苦的死亡。绝望的哥萨克军官开始在营地内自杀。母亲们将自己的孩子扔进德拉瓦河的泥水中，随后也跳了下去，选择死亡而不是落入苏联刽子手的手中。那些拒绝登上卡车的人被英国士兵用枪托和刺刀强行驱赶。这是西方民主史上最可耻的时刻之一。丘吉尔实际上犯下了反人类罪，牺牲了无助和无辜的人民。一旦移交，筛选过程立即开始。契卡（SMERSH）特工开始在火车车厢旁处决军官。哥萨克首领——克拉斯诺夫（Krasnov）、什库罗（Shkuro）等人——被带到莫斯科，并于1947年被绞死。数以万计的哥萨克妇女和儿童被驱逐到西伯利亚集中营，大多数人死于饥饿和残酷的强迫劳动。利恩茨的悲剧是一个痛苦的提醒，即“大政治”往往是用普通人的鲜血写成的。西方用与俄罗斯“和平共处”的幻觉换取了数万人的生命。丘吉尔和罗斯福天真地希望斯大林会“改变”，希望俄罗斯会成为一个愿意合作的“更好的国家”。我们还应该记住，类似的模式在最近的格鲁吉亚历史上也重演过，当时在“重启”的幻觉下，巴拉克·奥巴马实际上在2012年为“格鲁吉亚梦想”党的上台开了绿灯，许多人认为这是俄罗斯帝国体系的转移。今天，当一些人仍然谈论“大国利益”而忽视较小国家和群体的命运时，利恩茨的例子应该成为一个警告：与俄罗斯的任何妥协都要付出可怕的人力代价。苏联的宣传掩盖了利恩茨悲剧几十年，而西方则因羞愧而保持沉默。但历史真相，就像德拉瓦河的水一样，永远无法被掩埋。 推文

text: ⚡ 🇷🇺 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Navy launched a Neptune cruise missile strike tonight against the Atlant Aero plant in Taganrog, Rostov oblast. The factory is believed to be producing drone components. @wfwitness
text_zh: ⚡ 🇷🇺 🇺🇦 乌克兰海军今晚向罗斯托夫州塔甘罗格的 Atlant Aero 工厂发射了一枚海王星巡航导弹。据信该工厂正在生产无人机组件。 @wfwitness

text: ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 The Lebanese army said it has reopened key roads and partially restored bridges in the south, with further repairs underway after damage from Israeli strikes in recent weeks. @wfwitness
text_zh: ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 黎巴嫩军队表示，已重新开放了南部的主要道路并部分修复了桥梁，在近期遭受以色列袭击受损后，更多的修复工作正在进行中。 @wfwitness
